features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported third quarter 2021 worldwide sales of $23.3 billion, a 10.7% increase year-over-year. Net earnings for the quarter were $3.7 billion, with diluted earnings per share rising to $1.37, up from $1.33 a year ago. The Pharmaceutical segment saw significant growth, with sales increasing 13.2%, while the Medical Devices segment grew 7% despite challenges from the COVID-19 Delta variant.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in the recovery of the Medical Devices market, anticipating continued growth in procedures as COVID-19 restrictions ease. They tightened their full-year 2021 adjusted operational sales growth guidance to a range of 12.4% to 13%, reflecting strong performance across segments. The company is also preparing for the launch of new products, including a CAR-T therapy, and expects to maintain a robust pipeline of innovative medicines.
features.risks: The company highlighted ongoing uncertainties related to the COVID-19 pandemic, particularly the impact of the Delta variant on elective procedures and hospital staffing shortages. Additionally, litigation expenses related to talc claims and other legal matters pose financial risks. Supply chain constraints and inflationary pressures are also concerns that could affect operational performance.
features.key_drivers: Key drivers of growth included strong performance in the Pharmaceutical segment, particularly in immunology and oncology products, as well as recovery in the Medical Devices segment. The Consumer Health segment benefited from increased demand for over-the-counter medicines. The company continues to invest heavily in R&D, with a 20.5% increase in investment year-over-year, supporting future product launches and innovations.
